title: Xtremely Spicy Korean Kimchi Namul
description: A spicy, molten, and savory Korean dish made with green olives, perfect for a post-workout meal. This version is Jain-friendly, using no onions or garlic.
instructions: Rinse the cabbage and remove any tough stems or leaves. Cut into 1-inch pieces and soak in cold water for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the cabbage, then set aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine the green olives, gochugaru, fish sauce (if using), rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, and soy sauce.
Blend until smooth, then set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the blended mixture and the cabbage.
Mix well to combine, then cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, heat a small non-stick pan over medium heat and add the lacquer glaze.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the glaze is smooth and syrupy.
Serve the kimchi namul in individual portions, topped with the warm lacquer glaze.
Garnish with additional green olives, if desired.
